notes: Learn your class abilities from trainers every 2 levels.
Pick up two gathering professions early for easy gold (Skinning + Herbalism or Mining).
Start collecting Linen Cloth to level First Aid.
Collect any gear with Stamina or your primary stat (Strength, Agility, or Intellect).
Level 10: Choose your first talent specialization point.
Save gold for your first mount at level 20 (requires 4g + 1g for Apprentice Riding).
Consider joining a guild for social benefits and leveling assistance.
Visit your class trainer every 2 levels—don't skip abilities!
Keep First Aid maxed for your level using Linen and Wool Cloth.
Level 20: Purchase Apprentice Riding and your first mount (60% speed).
Dungeon: Shadowfang Keep (Alliance) or Deadmines (Horde) for gear upgrades.
Keep your professions maxed for your level—gathering professions earn good gold.
Start collecting Silk Cloth for mid-level First Aid bandages.
Consider picking up a crafting profession if you have enough gold.
Dungeon: Scarlet Monastery (all wings) for excellent XP and gear.
Level 30: Paladin and Warlock class mounts become available via quest.
Save gold for level 40 mount upgrade (requires ~50g total).
Keep Silk and Mageweave Cloth for First Aid—very useful at this level.
Stranglethorn Vale: Do the Green Hills of Stranglethorn pages quest for a big XP boost.
Level 40: Purchase Journeyman Riding (100% ground mount speed) for ~50g.
Dungeons: Zul'Farrak (Tanaris), Uldaman, and Maraudon for gear and XP.
Start collecting Mageweave and Runecloth for high-level First Aid.
Level 40+: Many class quests unlock—check your trainer for epic abilities.
Consider running Sunken Temple for the class-specific helm quest.
Dungeons: Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance for pre-raid gear.
Save gold for Cold Weather Flying training at level 77 (~1,000g).
Level 50: Druids can learn Flight Form via class quest.
Max out First Aid with Runecloth bandages—essential for all classes.
Complete Onyxia attunement questline if you plan to raid at 60.
Level 60: Train Expert Riding (150% flying) in Hellfire for ~250g + mount cost.
Level 62: Do Ring of Blood quest chain in Nagrand for huge XP and weapon.
Level 64+: Start Nagrand Nesingwary quest line for easy XP.
Level 68: You can head to Northrend (Borean Tundra or Howling Fjord).
Reputation: Prioritize Cenarion Expedition (flying mount discount) or your faction's main hub.
Dungeons: Hellfire Ramparts, Blood Furnace, Slave Pens for quick gear upgrades.
Level 68: Train Northrend professions at Valiance Keep (Alliance) or Warsong Hold (Horde).
Level 70: Train Artisan Riding (280% flying) in Dalaran for ~5,000g.
Level 77: Train Cold Weather Flying in Dalaran for ~1,000g (required for Storm Peaks/Icecrown).
Prioritize quest rewards with Hit Rating, Crit, or Attack/Spell Power.
Dungeons: Utgarde Keep, Nexus, Azjol-Nerub for leveling gear.
Reputation: Start Kirin Tor (Dalaran) or Wyrmrest Accord reputation for head enchants.
Level 77: Make Dalaran your home—it has portals to all major cities.
Must-do: Complete the Sons of Hodir questline in Storm Peaks for shoulder enchants.
Level 78+: Start daily quests for gold and reputation.
Icecrown: Do Argent Tournament dailies (level 77+) for mounts, pets, and gold.
Prepare for level 80: Get friendly with your faction's faction for head enchant.
Dungeons: Heroic 5-mans unlock at 80 for Badge of Heroism farming.
Congratulations! You've reached max level!
Priorities: Max out your professions, especially gathering for gold income.
Start running Heroic dungeons for Badge of Heroism gear (item level 200+).
Get friendly with Kirin Tor or Wyrmrest Accord for head enchants.
Complete Sons of Hodir to Honored for shoulder enchants (essential for raiding).
Join a raiding guild if interested in Naxxramas, Ulduar, or ICC progression.
Daily quests: Argent Tournament, Sons of Hodir, and Ebon Blade for gold.
Consider leveling an alt or farming old-world content for achievements!
